Title: Dosage differences in 12-OXOPHYTODIENOATE REDUCTASE genes modulate wheat root growth

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates root architecture and contributes to drought-related adaptation via modulation of root growth
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OPDA/4,5-ddh-JA reduction step in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates leading to JA and JA-Ile accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA, and 4,5-ddh-JA in the JA biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a sensitive key control point of the JA pathway; single-gene dosage changes altered root phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in distal seminal root region; higher at 6 DAG than 16 DAG; detected in 1RW roots and absent from 1RS because gene is in 1BS introgression; constitutive transgene also expressed in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize UBIQUITIN promoter (constitutive) in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher OPRIII-B1 dosage/expression promotes JA/JA-Ile accumulation and shortens seminal roots; loss-of-function mutation increases seminal root length
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPRIII-B1 was prioritized from the duplicated/deleted candidate region because it is root expressed and linked to JA biosynthesis. 2. A 32-bp frameshift loss-of-function mutation in OPRIII-B1 significantly increased seminal root length in 1RW. 3. Constitutive expression of OPRIII-B1 in Hahn-1RS reduced seminal root length. 4. OPRIII proteins function as active reductases on JA-pathway substrates and increased dosage/expression is associated with higher JA and JA-Ile and altered root architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; transgenic overexpression; root phenotyping in hydroponics; recombinant enzyme assay; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression reduced leaf length and aerial dry weight, indicating possible growth penalty; single knockout only partially restored long-root phenotype relative to 1RS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Genome editing or dosage fine-tuning of OPRIII-B1 could help optimize wheat root architecture and drought adaptation while maintaining breadmaking quality in recombinant 1RS/1BS backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic growth; water-limited field conditions discussed; ibuprofen inhibition of JA biosynthesis; MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA819072, PRJNA819073, PRJNA819075; Quant-seq PRJNA847262, PRJNA847590
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although direct JA/JA-Ile quantification was shown for genotype sets including 1RW and UBI::OPRIII-R5 rather than OPRIII-B1 alone, transcriptomic and pharmacological data support the same pathway connection.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates root architecture and contributes to water-stress adaptation through effects on root system development
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OPDA/4,5-ddh-JA reduction step in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates leading to JA and JA-Ile accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred NADPH-dependent reduction of JA-pathway oxylipin substrates as a subfamily III OPR enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of a dosage-sensitive control point in the JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Duplicated rye OPRIII genes showed higher expression in 1RW than in 1RS; root tip expression higher at 6 DAG than 16 DAG for most OPRIII genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function alleles in one duplicated OPRIII-R1 copy increase seminal root length in 1RW
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two independent OPRIII-R1 deletions (3-bp and 7-bp) causing premature truncation were recovered in 1RW. 2. Plants carrying either mutant allele had 17–23% longer seminal roots than 1RW controls. 3. These results show reduced OPRIII-R1 dosage promotes longer roots, consistent with reduced JA-pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 mutagenesis; amplicon sequencing/NGS genotyping; hydroponic root phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-copy editing did not fully restore the long-root phenotype of 1RS, suggesting multiple OPRIII copies contribute
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing rye-derived OPRIII-R1 copies in 1RS/1BS recombinant wheat could improve root elongation under drought-prone conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct recombinant enzyme assay was not reported specifically for OPRIII-R1, but functional assignment is strongly inferred from family membership and parallel genetics.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls root architecture, associated with root growth arrest, lateral root positioning, and adaptation to water limitation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OPDA/4,5-ddh-JA reduction step in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates leading to increased JA and JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred NADPH-dependent reduction of JA-pathway oxylipin substrates as a subfamily III OPR enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of a dosage-sensitive control point in the JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rye OPRIII genes are more highly expressed in 1RW than in 1RS due to duplication; transgene expression detected in leaves under maize UBI promoter
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize UBIQUITIN promoter (constitutive) in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression of OPRIII-R5 shortens seminal roots, promotes earlier lateral root emergence near the RAM, alters ROS distribution, and increases JA/JA-Ile accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UBI::OPRIII-R5 reduced seminal root length by 18–28% in both Hahn-1RS and Fielder backgrounds. 2. Overexpression caused secondary roots to emerge closer to the root apical meristem and restricted ROS distribution to distal root regions. 3. Root tips of UBI::OPRIII-R5 accumulated significantly more JA and JA-Ile than 1RS. 4. Transcriptome changes in UBI::OPRIII-R5 strongly paralleled those in 1RW, linking OPRIII dosage to JA and ROS-related pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; hydroponic root phenotyping; JA/JA-Ile metabolite profiling; ROS staining; transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression reduced leaf length and aerial dry weight, indicating non-root growth penalties under ectopic expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning cereal root architecture, but expression should likely be root-specific or dosage-optimized to avoid shoot penalties
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic growth; ibuprofen and MeJA pharmacology used to connect OPRIII effects to JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzyme assay was not performed specifically on R5 in this paper; causal link supported by overexpression phenotype, hormone measurements, and transcriptome similarity to 1RW.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates in the JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a functional OPRIII enzyme capable of reducing JA-pathway substrates in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant OPRIII-B2 significantly reduced OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA and 4,5-ddh-JA using NADPH. 2. OPRIII-B2 localized to the nucleus and cytoplasm outside the peroxisome. 3. These data support subfamily III OPR enzymes as contributors to JA biosynthesis in wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; transient GFP localization in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly edited or overexpressed for phenotype in this study, but experimentally validated biochemically as an active OPRIII enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates in the JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a functional OPRIII enzyme capable of reducing JA-pathway substrates in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant OPRIII-B3 significantly reduced OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA and 4,5-ddh-JA using NADPH. 2. OPRIII-B3 localized to the nucleus and cytoplasm outside the peroxisome. 3. These data support biochemical competence of wheat OPRIII enzymes in JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; transient GFP localization in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct root phenotype for B3 was reported in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR subfamily III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced oxylipin intermediates in the JA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a functional OPRIII enzyme capable of reducing JA-pathway substrates in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant OPRIII-A2 significantly reduced OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA and 4,5-ddh-JA using NADPH. 2. OPRIII-A2 localized to nucleus and cytoplasm outside peroxisomes. 3. These data support wheat OPRIII enzymes as non-peroxisomal contributors to JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; transient GFP localization in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a biochemically validated core OPRIII enzyme, although not directly tied to mutant root phenotypes in this paper.
